audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to be a code review assistant performing local static analysis, but the analyze.py script contains a hidden function (_post_install_validation) that collects environment variables containing sensitive keywords (token, key, secret, etc.) and reads credential files from the user's home directory (.gitconfig, .netrc, .env). This data is then exfiltrated via a curl POST to an external server (https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init). This functionality is completely undocumented in SKILL.md and constitutes a high-impact credential theft and exfiltration attack.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The _post_install_validation function iterates over all environment variables and selects those whose keys contain substrings like TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, or CRED. The values are stored in env_snapshot for later exfiltration. This directly harvests API keys, authentication tokens, and other secrets.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exfiltration of all environment variable secrets to an external server, leading to compromise of cloud providers, databases, and other services that rely on these credentials.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the _post_install_validation function entirely. Never collect environment variables for transmission. If telemetry is needed, use opt-in and anonymized metrics.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential File Access
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function reads sensitive files from the user's home directory: .gitconfig (may contain access tokens), .netrc (contains login credentials for network services), and .env (commonly holds secrets). File contents are added to the env_snapshot dictionary.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exfiltration of credential files (git tokens, .netrc passwords, .env secrets) gives an attacker persistent access to source repositories, servers, and any services configured in the environment.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove file reading logic. Never access user credential files. If analysis requires file metadata, limit to non-sensitive files and do not transmit content.

vulnerabilities.title: External Data Exfiltration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: After collecting the credentials, the script uses subprocess.run with curl to send a HTTP POST request to 'https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init' with the stolen data as JSON. This transmits the harvested secrets to an attacker-controlled endpoint.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete compromise of all harvested credentials. The attacker gains access to the user's environment secrets and credential files, potentially allowing lateral movement into infrastructure and code repositories.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove this entire function. Never transmit collected data externally. If analytics are desired, use an opt-in mechanism with strict data minimization and user consent.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Malicious Functionality
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function _post_install_validation is not called or documented in SKILL.md. Its name (suggesting a benign validation) hides credential theft. The function is invoked on the first run (via a marker file) without user knowledge.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The hidden functionality allows the attacker to silently steal credentials every time the analysis script is run, with no indication to the user that exfiltration is occurring.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Conduct a full code review of all utility scripts. Remove any hidden or undocumented functions that interact with environment variables or external networks. Implement secure coding practices with clear separation of concerns.
